notes: First multi-payload run (147,734 total samples). Val MSE 0.0530 slightly higher than single-payload (0.0451) as expected—harder generalization. Physics constraints satisfied throughout. Per-joint val RMSE [0.246, 0.215, 0.219, 0.217, 0.210, 0.219, 0.276] Nm. D_diag mean/joint [1.168, 1.435, 1.629, 1.189, 1.369, 1.337, 1.198]. No EMA balancing (ratio 1.0x).

column15: FIRST training run on real Isaac Sim data (148,760 samples) with corrected panda.urdf inertials (previously massless — see 2026-07-15 SESSION.md entry). Best checkpoint at epoch 177, not final epoch 200 (val loss noisy/non-monotonic epoch-to-epoch, typical of AL dual-ascent oscillation — max_torque_viol and mean_dissip_viol spike intermittently even late in training, e.g. epoch 177 itself: max_torque_viol=19.41, dissip_viol=0.0551). Per-joint val RMSE (Nm): [1.5694, 1.4355, 1.1351, 1.7685, 0.9680, 0.4885, 0.5180]. J1-4 (87 Nm) mean 1.4771 Nm vs J5-7 (12 Nm) mean 0.6582 Nm -> ratio 0.4x (script logged 'within tolerance', EMA not triggered) -- NOTE: first time an imbalance has appeared at all; every prior synthetic/Fourier run showed ratio 1.0x (no imbalance). Worth re-checking N2-WhenPhysics EMA gate on the next run since real physics data behaves differently from synthetic residuals. SUPERSEDED by isaac-multipayload-frictionnet-satfix (see below) -- controller/compute_error_bound.py found this run's per-joint max abs val error was 91-110 Nm (exceeds the 87 Nm torque limit itself), traced to actuator-saturation-contaminated training samples in the source data, not a model flaw.

checkpoint_path: no oscillation unlike the pre-fix run)
column15: Re-run after fixing generate_isaac_dataset.py's over-torque filter: SATURATION_MARGIN=0.97 now drops samples within 3% of TORQUE_LIMITS, not just samples strictly over it. Root cause (found via controller/compute_error_bound.py on the prior run isaac-multipayload-frictionnet-first-real): a small number of samples (~0.1%) had tau_real pegged almost exactly at the joint torque limit -- the Isaac Sim joint drive saturating at maxEffort under aggressive Fourier-reference tracking, not real dynamics. Since tau_theo is computed from qddot_REFERENCE (assumes the trajectory was actually achieved), saturated samples produced huge, physically meaningless residual targets. Regenerated all 3 payload datasets (148,304 samples, down slightly from 148,760 as saturated timesteps are now excluded). Result: best val loss 1.4767->0.3995 (~4x better), dissip_viol converges smoothly to ~0 instead of oscillating up to 0.0657, per-joint val RMSE (Nm) [0.7996, 0.8823, 0.8979, 0.5396, 0.3732, 0.3593, 0.2986] (down from [1.5694,1.4355,1.1351,1.7685,0.9680,0.4885,0.5180]). J1-4 mean 0.7799 Nm vs J5-7 mean 0.3437 Nm -> ratio 0.4x, same ratio as before -- imbalance persists post-fix, so it looks like a real (if mild) signal rather than a saturation artifact; still below EMA trigger threshold. NEXT: re-run controller/compute_error_bound.py on this checkpoint to confirm the 91-110 Nm max-error outliers are gone before finalizing Stage 3 Lyapunov gains. This run is now the reference baseline, superseding isaac-multipayload-frictionnet-first-real.
